# Implementation Plan: FinSync macOS Desktop App

**Branch**: `001-macos-desktop-app` | **Date**: 2026-05-05 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/Users/marciomorais/Sites/finSync-MacOS/specs/001-macos-desktop-app/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by the `/speckit.plan` workflow and stops before task generation.

## Summary

Build FinSync macOS as a native, installable desktop application that authenticates the account owner against the existing Supabase project, reads already-processed financial data directly from the existing data model, and provides dashboard, imports, accounts, transactions, review, categories, KPIs, forecast, and redacted audit views. The app will not create a new backend, will not process OFX/PDF locally, and will only mutate classification-review outcomes that the existing product already supports.

Technical approach: create a SwiftUI macOS app backed by a small domain/data layer around `supabase-swift`, local protected session/cache storage, deterministic view models for dashboard/KPI aggregation, and guarded mutation flows for classification review conflicts.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Swift 6.x using the current stable Xcode toolchain available on the target macOS development machine  
**Primary Dependencies**: SwiftUI/AppKit integration; Supabase Swift client (`supabase-swift`); Swift Package Manager; XCTest and XCUITest  
**Storage**: Existing Supabase Postgres project as source of truth; local protected cache for recent financial data; Keychain for secrets/session material  
**Testing**: XCTest for domain/data/view-model logic, XCUITest for critical macOS flows, contract-style tests with mocked Supabase responses  
**Target Platform**: macOS 14+ desktop app, locally installable  
**Project Type**: Native macOS desktop app  
**Performance Goals**: Authenticated user can reach dashboard insight in <=30 seconds; common lists with up to 1,000 visible records show loading/result/error in <=3 seconds on normal connectivity; review action completes in <=3 interactions after opening queue  
**Constraints**: No backend creation; no packaged web app; no local OFX/PDF processing; all financial reads filtered by `account_owner_id`; no financial data before auth; cache must be protected and cleared on logout; aggregate totals must not mix currencies  
**Scale/Scope**: Single-account-owner desktop experience, approximately 8 primary screens plus details, optimized for personal recurring finance usage and datasets in the low thousands of visible rows per screen

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

The project constitution at `/Users/marciomorais/Sites/finSync-MacOS/.specify/memory/constitution.md` still contains placeholder principles and no enforceable project-specific gates. No constitution violations are identified. Planning will apply the feature spec requirements as the active quality gate:

- Native installable macOS app, not a web wrapper.
- Existing Supabase data model remains the source of truth.
- No new backend, no local document processing, no mutation of immutable financial facts.
- User/account-owner isolation, masking, protected cache, and redacted audit display are mandatory.
- Classification corrections must preserve history and leave one active classification.

Post-design re-check: PASS. Phase 1 artifacts preserve the same constraints and introduce no additional backend or out-of-scope processing.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-macos-desktop-app/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   ├── supabase-data-access.md
│   └── ui-state-contracts.md
└── tasks.md
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
FinSyncMacOS/
├── FinSyncMacOS.xcodeproj
├── FinSyncMacOS/
│   ├── App/
│   ├── Core/
│   │   ├── Auth/
│   │   ├── Cache/
│   │   ├── Config/
│   │   ├── Errors/
│   │   └── Supabase/
│   ├── Domain/
│   │   ├── Models/
│   │   ├── Repositories/
│   │   └── UseCases/
│   ├── Features/
│   │   ├── Dashboard/
│   │   ├── Imports/
│   │   ├── Accounts/
│   │   ├── Transactions/
│   │   ├── Review/
│   │   ├── Categories/
│   │   ├── KPIs/
│   │   ├── Forecast/
│   │   └── Audit/
│   └── Resources/
├── FinSyncMacOSTests/
│   ├── Domain/
│   ├── Repositories/
│   └── ViewModels/
└── FinSyncMacOSUITests/
    ├── AuthFlowTests.swift
    ├── DashboardFlowTests.swift
    └── ReviewFlowTests.swift
```

**Structure Decision**: Use a single native macOS application project with feature folders for UI/view models and shared domain/repository layers. This keeps the first desktop release simple while isolating Supabase access, cache/session behavior, business rules, and screen-specific presentation.

## Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations or complexity exceptions are required.
